steps.title: Track the passage argument
steps.content: The passage explains that Sophon calls his films sleep inducing, but it rejects the idea that this is self-deprecation. Sleepiness is presented as an intentional effect of his slow-cinema work, not as proof that the films fail.

steps.title: Reject distorted summaries
steps.content: Choice A invents a claim about audiences failing to enjoy the films. Choice B creates an unsupported split between viewers. Choice C says slow cinema generally induces anxiety, which the passage does not claim. The main idea is that Sophon's description should not be read as an admission of failure. The answer is D.
